spec_match.rationale: The page no longer claims obsolete axis-only wrapping or current native 2D grid support; it now uses listbox focusgroup syntax plus a JS row/column helper.

interactive.rationale: The pickers keep click and keyboard selection logic in source, but chrome-devtools-mcp was unavailable to verify the visual grid interaction.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature and concept copy now state that native 2D grid behavior is outside the current Chrome 150 focusgroup contract.

edge_cases.rationale: Unsupported focusgroup fallback and current-token listbox semantics are covered, but native grid focusgroup remains a future open design area.

references.rationale: The page links to ChromeStatus and the current scoped Open UI explainer.

design_system.rationale: Picker surfaces keep the shared CSS system, clear focus outlines, and visual color swatches as demo content.

openQuestions.title: Verify visual picker keyboard fallback
openQuestions.detail: Use chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome 150+ to Tab into both pickers, exercise Left/Right/Up/Down, select with Enter/Space, confirm aria-selected/focusgroupstart updates, and inspect console/network.

openQuestions.title: Track native grid focusgroup separately
openQuestions.detail: If Chrome later exposes a real grid/manual-grid focusgroup behavior, build or restore a native 2D concept instead of treating visual-grid row math as a Chrome 150 contract.

summary: Static repair reframed this as a current listbox-focusgroup visual grid with JS row/column movement, removing the stale axis-only and future-grid-token claim.
